scenario: Vertex Logistics, a carrier handling 22000 shipments per day, evaluates Camunda 8 TypeScript clients. One service still targets a Camunda 8.7 broker, another streams jobs via gRPC, and a third is a brand new Camunda 8.8 microservice that uses only the Orchestration Cluster API. The architect must decide for which of these the @camunda8/sdk package is the documented choice.
question: Which use case does the SDK page list as a reason to use the @camunda8/sdk package?

options.text: Building a brand new web browser SPA that talks to Camunda 8.8 only via the Orchestration Cluster REST API and that has no need for gRPC job streaming on the client side
Migrating an existing application to the 8.8 Orchestration Cluster API, where the page lists migration of an existing app among the reasons to pick @camunda8/sdk
Writing a brand new Camunda 8.8 service from scratch that only consumes the Orchestration Cluster API and never targets a Camunda 8.7 broker or gRPC job streaming endpoint
Standing up a brand new dashboard that runs entirely in the browser and reads Operate data via the Orchestration Cluster API, with no server-side Node.js worker at all

a.text: Incorrect. The SDK runs in Node.js and cannot run in a web browser, so a browser SPA is not a documented use case for @camunda8/sdk.

b.text: Correct. The docs list migrating an existing application to the 8.8 Orchestration Cluster API among the reasons to choose @camunda8/sdk, alongside gRPC job streaming and targets of 8.7 or earlier.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs steer brand new 8.8+ projects without gRPC or v1 API needs toward the Orchestration Cluster API TypeScript client instead of @camunda8/sdk.

d.text: Incorrect. The @camunda8/sdk package does not run in a browser due to technical limitations; a browser dashboard is not a documented use case for it.

explanation: The page lists three reasons to pick @camunda8/sdk: needing the gRPC API for job streaming, targeting a server on 8.7 or earlier, or migrating an existing application to the 8.8 Orchestration Cluster API. New 8.8+ projects without those needs are pointed at the Orchestration Cluster API TypeScript client.
